interactions.summary: The host asks whether Christian Zionism or evangelical Christianity is simply a tool of Zionism, and Jiang reverses the direction by saying Zionism may be a tool of Christian Zionists.
Jiang sketches a chain from Frankists to infiltrated Freemasons, Christian Zionism, American conquest, and then political Zionism.

Dugin answers the Russia/Zionism question by tracing fifth monarchism, Dutch Protestant circles, greater Israel, Orthodox views of Judaism, Putin's realism, and a current eschatological reading of Israel/Trump aggression.

speaker_notes.note: Diarization labels are not reliable in this multi-speaker interview; speaker attribution is inferred from conversational role and named address.

claims.claim: Jiang argues that political Zionism can be read as an instrument created by Christian Zionists to fulfill their eschatology, not only the reverse.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim made in this April 13, 2026 interview.

claims.claim: Dugin traces a 17th-century fifth-monarchy and Dutch Protestant context for early greater-Israel thinking, linking it to John Locke and modern Israeli maximalism.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim made in this April 13, 2026 interview.

claims.claim: Dugin says Russian Orthodoxy never had a Christian Zionist equivalent: Judaism is respected as a separate faith, while chosenness after Christ is rejected theologically.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim made in this April 13, 2026 interview.

claims.claim: Dugin says Putin is politically realist and pragmatic toward Israel, while Orthodox Russians project a catechonic function onto him.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim made in this April 13, 2026 interview.

claims.claim: Dugin says Israel's current aggression and Trump's sacrilegious imagery make the moment look eschatological even if Russia does not insist on that frame.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim made in this April 13, 2026 interview.

signature_moments.moment: Jiang flips the usual tool relation: Zionism may be the tool built by Christian Zionists.
signature_moments.source_phrase: Zionism is a tool of Christian Zionists
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It is a provocative model that the read should treat as Jiang's claim, not as established fact.

signature_moments.moment: Putin is described as a pragmatic realist onto whom Orthodox Russians project the catechon.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This distinction prevents the read from confusing Putin's own realism with Dugin's theological projection.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Dugin uses this for an early-modern Protestant idea of a fifth global empire that he links to greater Israel.
The restraining power that Orthodox Russians project onto Putin as keeper against Antichrist.

uncertainty_notes.note: Jiang's Frankist/Freemason chain is presented speculatively and should be attributed as his model rather than stated as settled history.
